# IMDb Top 250 Monomyths

- Parent README: [../README.md](../README.md)
- Seed schema: [../Monomyth.gg](../Monomyth.gg)
- Test suite: [imdb-top-250-monomyths.test.gg](./imdb-top-250-monomyths.test.gg)
- Generator: [../../../scripts/generate-imdb-top-250-monomyths.ts](../../../scripts/generate-imdb-top-250-monomyths.ts)

This directory contains 250 generated `.gg` modules, one per title in the
static IMDb Top 250 snapshot dated `2026-01-01`.

## Getting Started

- **What:** example material for the imdb top 250 monomyths topic.
- **Why:** it gives a concrete graph or fixture before you need to understand the full runtime implementation.
- **How:** read one small example first, then run the matching test or benchmark only when you need executable confirmation.
- **Next:** use examples to learn shape; use source READMEs to change behavior.

The source surface is [List Challenges](https://www.listchallenges.com/imdb2026),
which mirrors a fixed 2026 IMDb chart snapshot and is automation-accessible
when direct IMDb chart fetches are WAF-blocked.

Each file instantiates the same coarse departure/initiation/return topology
from [Monomyth.gg](../Monomyth.gg). These are corpus witnesses, not
screenplay-close proofs that every listed film is a strict Campbell fit.

## Naming

- Files follow `NNN-<slug>.gg`, where `NNN` is the chart rank in the
  `2026-01-01` snapshot.
- The suite groups witnesses by the seven source pages used to harvest the
  snapshot.

## Representative Files

- [001-the-shawshank-redemption.gg](./001-the-shawshank-redemption.gg)
- [128-the-truman-show.gg](./128-the-truman-show.gg)
- [250-andhadhun.gg](./250-andhadhun.gg)
